title: VAP Intent Envelope (per-call)
description: Lightweight per-call message bound to an accepted Scope Commitment by session_id. Carries the rationale for THIS call. Checked deterministically against the active commitment (scope/budget/policy); semantic (LLM) re-judgement is invoked only when the C5 risk scorer crosses threshold. Note: full chain-of-thought MUST NOT be sent here — only a minimal structured intent claim.

session_id.description: References the active Scope Commitment. The server MUST reject calls whose session_id has no accepted commitment.

intent.description: The verifiable 'why' for this specific call. Minimal and checkable — NOT raw reasoning trace.

rationale.description: Short justification linking this call to the session goal (e.g. 'Ticket SUP-9931 reports a double-charge; refunding the duplicate').

expected_effect.description: The bounded outcome the agent expects (e.g. 'one refund <= $120 to original payment method'). Used to check args are consistent with stated intent.

step.description: Advisory position in the current plan. Informational; drift is NOT defined as deviation from step order.

sensitivity.description: Self-declared risk class. Drives the C5 risk scorer. Servers MAY override based on the tool being called.

reasoning_digest.description: Optional hash of the agent's full reasoning for this call, retained client-side / in audit. Lets auditors later verify a disclosed CoT matches, without transmitting it. Format 'sha256:<hex>'.

call.description: The actual tool invocation (mirrors the underlying MCP tools/call).

tool.description: Tool name. MUST satisfy the active commitment's tools_allow \ tools_deny.

arguments.description: Tool arguments. Validated against the tool's own schema. Argument-level constraints, if any, are enforced by the deployment-local operator policy hook (C4), not by the VAP protocol.

signature.description: Optional JWS over the canonicalized envelope (JCS). REQUIRED when the server advertises require_signed=true.
